data.title: Euro Area retail sales rise 2.3% in April, GDP expands 1.5% in Q1
data.description:
• Retail sales in the Euro Area increased 2.30% in April of 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

  • Retail sales increased 0.1% in April of 2025 over the previous month. 

  • The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the Euro Area expanded 1.5% in the first quarter of 2025 over the same quarter of the previous year. 

  • The Gross Domestic Product expanded 0.6% in the first quarter of 2025 over the previous quarter. 

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Stamper Oil & Gas plans to buy shares of BISP Exploration
Tracer Biotechnologies and QIAGEN to advance blood-based MRD testing for solid tumors
data.description:
Tracer Biotechnologies Friday reported a strategic partnership with QIAGEN (NYSE:QGEN) to co-develop and commercialize minimal residual disease (MRD) assays for solid tumors on QIAGEN’s QIAcuity digital PCR platform.

This partnership seeks to provide highly sensitive, cost-effective, and decentralized MRD testing solutions, allowing oncologists to track cancer recurrence and tailor treatment plans using minimally invasive blood samples.

data.title: Stock index futures up over possible thaw in Trump-Musk feud; eyes on key labor data
data.description:
Stock index futures gained on Friday, as Elon Musk indicated he would take steps to ease tensions with President Donald Trump, and investors set their eyes on the big upcoming payrolls report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.4%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.91%.

The indication of a potential thaw in tensions between Musk and Trump comes after a public feud that erupted between them on Thursday. Earlier, their clash escalated when the president threatened to terminate Musk’s government contracts, leading to a 14% drop in Tesla’s (TSLA) shares.

All eyes will be on the May nonfarm payrolls report. The consensus estimate for 127K new jobs in May indicates slower growth from the 177K jobs added in April, according to the Department of Labor's initial estimate.

Meanwhile, the unemployment rate is expected to stay at 4.2%, according to the consensus of economists.

"The May employment report is due, with the regular reminders that this data has become increasingly unreliable in recent years, and average earnings are not wages. This month’s data will correct errors that crept into last month’s data," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Signs of weakness in restaurant and leisure travel sectors mean fewer lower paid workers may be employed, raising average earnings without affecting wages. But, the Federal Reserve’s “data dependency” means the labor market is seen as a trigger for policy action, Donovan added. 

Wall Street closed in the red as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid Trump and Musk's verbal spat. 

"Markets had a volatile session yesterday, as they grappled with a barrage of news that each pushed in different directions. Those included positive U.S.-China headlines amid a call between Trump and Xi, a hawkish ECB decision, and more weak data from the U.S. But the most remarkable was an extraordinary war of words between Trump and Elon Musk that ultimately left risk assets losing ground," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The trade headlines outweighed an initial negative reaction to the latest weekly U.S. jobless claims, which added to fears that the U.S. labor market was finally deteriorating after Liberation Day, Reid added. 

Trump on Thursday said that he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

data.title: LME intervenes to make Mercuria roll huge aluminum position - report
data.description:
The London Metal Exchange has compelled Mercuria Energy Group to lend out its huge position in aluminum to other traders to reduce risks to the market, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The LME took action after Mercuria’s position in the June contract was consistently far bigger than the aluminum inventories in the exchange’s warehouse system — most of which are also already owned by Mercuria, the people said.

That means that if Mercuria were to hold the position to expiry, those on the other side of the market might struggle to find sufficient aluminum to deliver against their short positions, the report added.

Potentially relevant tickers include (XME), (CPER), (OTC:JJCTF), (AA), (CENX), (KALU)

data.title: WEC Energy upsizes and prices $775M senior convertible notes
data.description:
• WEC Energy Group (NYSE:WEC) priced $775M in aggregate principal amount of its 3.375% convertible senior notes due 2028 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.
  • The offering was upsized from the previously announced offering of $700M convertible notes.
  • The initial purchasers have an option to purchase, within a 13-day period up to an additional $125M convertible notes. T
  • The offering is expected to close on June 10, 2025.
  • Interest on the convertible notes will be paid semiannually, at a rate of 3.375% per annum. 
  • Net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of short-term indebtedness.

data.title: Séché Environnement signs unilateral offer to acquire Groupe Flamme for ~€300M
Canadian National Railway to raise C$1 billion via debt
data.description:
Canadian National Railway (TSX:CNR:CA) (NYSE:CNI) announced a public debt offering of C$500M 3.500% notes due 2030, and C$500M 4.200% notes due 2035.

CN expects to close the offering on June 10, 2025.

CN plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, which may include the redemption and refinancing of outstanding indebtedness, share repurchases, acquisitions and other business opportunities.

The debt offering is being made in Canada under CN’s base shelf prospectus dated April 2, 2024.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: TSLA, DOCU, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher Friday morning as investors looked ahead to the key U.S. jobs report, which could offer fresh insight into the health of the economy. Sentiment was also stirred by an escalating public feud between President Donald Trump and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) +6% – Shares looked to rebound after Thursday’s 14% plunge, which wiped $152B off its market cap, its largest single-day loss, dragging it below the $1T mark to $916B. The selloff followed a public feud between CEO Elon Musk and President Trump, who threatened to revoke federal contracts over disagreements tied to the spending bill.

Biggest stock losers

  • DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU) -17% – Shares tumbled after the electronic signature company lowered its full-year billings guidance, overshadowing stronger-than-expected Q1 results. For Q2, DocuSign expects revenue between $777M and $781M (vs. $774.47M estimate), with subscription revenue between $760M and $764M and billings in the range of $757M to $767M. For FY2026, billings are now projected at $3.285B to $3.339B, slightly below the prior forecast of $3.3B to $3.354B. However, full-year revenue guidance was raised to $3.151B to $3.163B (vs. a $3.13B estimate). The board also expanded the share buyback program by $1B, raising the total to $1.4B.
  • lululemon athletica (NASDAQ:LULU) -22% – Shares plunged after the retailer posted weaker-than-expected comparable sales and trimmed its FY2025 profit outlook, excluding any tariff-related impacts despite heavy import exposure to Southeast Asia and China. For Q2, revenue is expected to be between $2.535B and $2.56B (7–8% Y/Y growth), just shy of the $2.57B consensus, with EPS projected at $2.85 to $2.90, much below the $3.34 consensus. FY2025 revenue guidance remains at $11.15B–$11.30B (5–7% growth) in line with the $11.24B consensus, while EPS is now forecast at $14.58–$14.78, down from $14.95–$15.15 prior and below the $15.03 estimate.
  • Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) -4% – Shares slipped despite delivering better-than-expected FQ2 results and upbeat guidance. Revenue rose 20% Y/Y to $15B, with semiconductor solutions at $8.41B and infrastructure software at $6.60B. For Q3, Broadcom guided revenue to ~$15.8B (vs. $15.75B est.), with adjusted EBITDA at ~66% of sales. CEO Hock Tan highlighted continued AI strength, forecasting AI chip revenue of $5.1B in Q3, marking 10 straight quarters of growth.

data.title: CyberArk Software prices $1.1B convertible senior notes
data.description:
• CyberArk Software (NASDAQ:CYBR) priced $1.1B aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.
  • The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $750M aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • Initial purchasers of the notes have a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $150 million aggregate principal amount of the notes.
  • The sale of the notes to the initial purchasers is expected to settle on June 10, 2025.
  • The net proceeds will be ~$1.07B (or $1.22B if the initial purchasers exercise their option to purchase additional notes).
  • Approximately $96.8M of the net proceeds will pay the cost of the capped call transactions, and the remaining net proceeds will be for working capital or other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price traded 1.1% lower on Thursday during after-market hours of trading.
